synthesis_text: The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include coughing, fever, and loss of smell and taste. Other frequently reported symptoms are fatigue, headaches, nasal congestion, muscle pain, and breathing difficulties, particularly in moderate to severe cases. Loss of taste combined with loss of smell is reported in up to 88% of cases, and shortness of breath is also a notable symptom associated with the disease [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00011][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00012][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00001].

The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include fever, cough, and extreme tiredness (fatigue). Other frequently reported symptoms are dry cough, shortness of breath, loss of taste or smell, congestion, runny nose, sore throat, headaches, body or muscle aches, and digestive symptoms such as upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea. Symptoms typically appear 2 to 14 days after exposure to the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00002].

The most common symptoms of COVID-19 include fever, cough, and tiredness [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Other symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain or pressure, muscle or body aches, headache, loss of taste or smell, confusion, sore throat, congestion or runny nose, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, abdominal pain, and skin rashes [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Infants may also experience difficulty feeding as a symptom [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00001].
